dividends.name: Cash Dividends from LVMH and Dior SE

other_income.name: Private Equity Yield and Management Fees

strategic_acquisitions.name: Media and Sports Assets (Paris FC & Editions Croque Futur)

2026.methodology: Income calculation: Total LVMH shares outstanding is approx 496M. Arnault family owns 248M shares. At approx 14.04 USD per share (converted from 13 EUR in Jan 2026), the gross dividend income from LVMH alone is 3.482B USD. Spending reflects the continued strategy of stock consolidation and a major pivot to AI. Aglaé Ventures participated in a 1.03B USD round for Advanced Machine Intelligence (AMI) in March 2026. Projections include the finalization of the Paris FC acquisition and the expansion of the media portfolio via the acquisition of Editions Croque Futur.

dividends.name: Cash Dividends from LVMH and Dior SE

sports_and_culture.name: Paris FC Investment and Cultural Support

2025.methodology: Income is calculated based on the total dividend distribution for the 2024 fiscal year paid in 2025, converted to USD. Total dividend was approx 14.04 USD per share. Spending reflects the documented 1.6B USD spent on LVMH share repurchases to increase family control over 50%, alongside Aglaé Ventures' continued 300M+ USD commitment to AI startups and the acquisition of a majority stake in Paris FC.

asset_yield.name: Real Estate and Private Investments Yield

philanthropy.name: Notre Dame Restoration & Paris Olympics Sponsorship

personal_investments.name: Luxury Real Estate and Private Assets

2024.methodology: Income reflects the realized cash dividends (approx 1.08B USD) paid to the Arnault family holdings (Agache). Total net worth fluctuated negatively by approximately 32B USD due to LVMH stock performance, but this is unrealized loss. Spending is calculated based on publicly announced investments in AI startups (approx 300M USD via Aglaé) and major social/philanthropic commitments including the 216M USD (200M EUR) pledge for Notre Dame and Olympic contributions.
